texto: [On Gottfried Wilhelm Leibniz]The answer is unknowable, but it may not be unreasonable to see him, at least in theological terms, as essentially a deist. He is a determinist: there are no miracles (the events so called being merely instances of infrequently occurring natural laws); Christ has no real role in the system; we live forever, and hence we carry on after our deaths, but then everything — every individual substance — carries on forever.
explicacao: Citação de Peter Loptson sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “[On Gottfried Wilhelm Leibniz]The answer is unknowable, but it may not be unreasonable to see him…” articula vida, deism, determinist em registro de vida, com relevância para contextos como luto e determinist.
